intro: Zeruah was the widowed mother of Jeroboam son of Nebat, who became the first king of the northern kingdom of Israel after the division of the monarchy (1 Kings 11:26). She is mentioned only in this single verse which identifies Jeroboam's parentage. Her status as a widow at the time of Jeroboam's rise is noted, perhaps to explain his ambition and self-reliance. The name means leprous or full of ringworm, though this may be a descriptive designation applied later rather than a given name. She is one of a number of widowed mothers in the Old Testament whose sons rose to prominence.
